items.title: Implement openingRules compatibility adapter
acceptance: Legacy openingHours maps into openingRules
No regressions in existing saved locations

items.title: Extend location schema with opening metadata
acceptance: Normalized locations include metadata fields for opening-hours provenance
Schema includes opening-rules coverage metadata without breaking existing consumers

items.title: Harden backward compatibility field adapter
acceptance: Legacy fields map into normalized openingHours/openingHoursText and note fields
Legacy duration and priority aliases map to normalized duration and priority
Normalization remains safe for existing and new location payloads

items.title: Add feasibility-aware scheduling in TSPSolver
acceptance: Solver can wait for open windows
Solver never schedules finishing after close
Unscheduled stops include statusReason

items.title: Show wait/closed/unscheduled badges in itinerary UI
Add trip-form opening-hours editor controls
acceptance: Each trip location card shows opening-hours text on its own line
Opening start/end times are editable directly from the location card
Edited opening hours persist in trip-location state used by optimization

items.title: Tune opening-hours parsing against London POI data
acceptance: Parser accepts 24:00 end-times used by POI records
Parser accepts alternate range separators such as en-dash and 'to'
Normalized openingHours resolves more POIs from real dataset hours text

items.title: Expose opening-hours provenance and placeholder status
acceptance: Each location shows whether hours came from POI source data, user edit, or fallback/default
Locations without trustworthy source hours are labeled clearly instead of appearing fully authoritative
Time-constrained optimization still works with the clarified provenance model

items.title: Enforce strict budget cap in priority mode
acceptance: Priority-mode output itinerary is hard-capped to the configured time budget
Any overflow branch nodes are dropped with exceeds-time-budget reason
Solver metadata budgetUsed/budgetRemaining reflects strict capped output

items.title: Improve dropped-stop reason quality
acceptance: Dropped stop reasons distinguish opening-window conflicts from time-budget overflow
Budget overflow after opening-hour waiting uses a dedicated status reason
Itinerary UI renders user-readable labels for the new status reason

items.title: Upgrade priority mode to DFS backtracking with pruning
acceptance: Priority mode explores multi-stop combinations using depth-first backtracking
Branch pruning limits search using optimistic priority bounds and visited-state memoization
Results remain budget and opening-window feasible

items.title: Evaluate transit providers for London Ontario
Define normalized transit response contract
Implement getTransitRouteEstimate adapter
Wire transit branch into routing flow
acceptance: Transit route estimation receives trip schedule date/time context
Planner route panel renders transit-specific details when transit legs are available
Walk and car route estimation behavior remains unchanged

items.title: Graceful transit fallback and unavailable messaging
acceptance: Transit fallback responses include user-readable notice messages
Unavailable provider states are surfaced clearly in planner UI
Fallback behavior remains non-breaking and returns usable route estimates

items.title: Restore visible planner route panel for transit estimates
acceptance: Trip form visibly renders route estimate details again when a route is available
Transit estimates show provider, schedule-aware status, notices, and up to four transit legs
Walk and car estimate rendering remains intact

items.title: Expand normalized transit response with schedule metadata
acceptance: Normalized transit results include transfer/wait/walk metadata needed by planner and itinerary UI
Transit leg payload preserves route labels and stop names where provider data supplies them
Existing walk/car route estimate consumers remain backward-compatible

items.title: Add cached transit leg cost provider for optimization
acceptance: Optimization flow can request transit travel cost between arbitrary stop pairs at a departure time
Transit leg requests are cached to avoid redundant provider calls within one optimization run
Provider failure returns a safe degraded result instead of breaking optimization

items.title: Use schedule-aware transit costs in itinerary optimization
acceptance: Transit optimization no longer relies on a flat transit speed heuristic
Inter-stop travel times reflect requested departure time and provider itinerary results
Walk and car optimization behavior remains regression-safe

items.title: Render transit leg summaries in itinerary window
acceptance: Transit itineraries show per-leg mode and origin/destination labels between stops
Fallback and unavailable states remain understandable in itinerary output
Non-transit itinerary rendering is unchanged

items.title: Document OTP environment, operations, and transit verification flow
acceptance: README or onboarding docs describe transit-specific environment variables
Docs capture OTP availability expectations, timeout behavior, and fallback behavior
Verification steps cover OTP success, timeout, and no-itinerary scenarios

items.title: Add runtime transit settings controls
acceptance: Settings window includes a mock transit toggle that changes runtime routing behavior
Settings window includes an OTP base URL input persisted in browser storage
Transit service honors settings overrides without requiring code changes

items.title: Replace mock road fallback with OSRM
items.notes: Superseded by product decision to keep Mapbox-based real routing path and avoid redundant OSRM integration.

items.title: Add weighted fuzzy scoring for local search
acceptance: Local ranking uses explicit weighted scoring by field (name/address/note)
Token-level fuzzy similarity improves tolerance to minor misspellings
Custom nodes retain ranking preference over equivalent POI matches

items.title: Improve travel-method active state visibility
acceptance: Selected travel method uses a distinct high-contrast active style
Active method remains visually obvious versus inactive options

items.title: Add settings/help panel
acceptance: Settings and Help dock buttons open in-app windows
Panels include core routing, optimization, and usage guidance

items.title: Add address enrichment to map context actions
acceptance: POI right-click add preserves source address
Empty-map context add and saved-location creation reverse geocode full address

items.title: Replace settings/help with actionable Time Constrained settings
acceptance: Help window is removed from the dock flow
Settings window includes actionable controls instead of static guidance blocks
Settings includes per-stop break-time input used by optimization flow

items.title: Move daily availability controls from Trip Form to Settings
acceptance: Wake/sleep (daily availability) controls are removed from Trip Form UI
Daily availability controls are available under Time Constrained Mode settings
Existing persistence for wake/sleep values remains regression-safe

items.title: Persist saved locations in workspace folder and load on startup
items.notes: Superseded by product decision to use browser-only persistence for saved locations instead of workspace root file writes.
acceptance: Saved locations are read from a designated workspace data folder at app startup
Save/edit/delete operations update the same folder-backed data source
When folder-backed persistence is unavailable, user sees a clear fallback/limitation message

items.title: Fix priority-mode add-order bias
acceptance: Priority solver no longer seeds from first-added location
Higher-priority feasible location can be selected first

items.title: Account initial travel before prioritized first stop
acceptance: Priority mode includes travel from first location anchor when another stop is seeded first
First stop arrival time reflects initial travel and opening-window wait when applicable

items.title: Show initial travel leg before first stop
acceptance: Itinerary shows travel time before first stop when seeded first stop differs from start anchor
User can visually confirm initial travel is accounted for

items.title: Add lookahead priority combination scoring
acceptance: Priority mode can favor two feasible lower-priority stops over one high-priority stop when combined value is better
Selection remains constrained by opening windows and time budget

items.title: Inline edit saved-location name and note in cards
acceptance: Saved-location cards display each location note directly in the card
Name and note are edited by clicking the text directly instead of using a separate edit button
Cards show a muted default note placeholder when no custom note exists so users can add one later

items.title: Set start/destination from saved nodes adds to trip
acceptance: Choosing Set Start from a saved-location card also adds that location to trip locations if missing
Choosing Set Destination from a saved-location card also adds that location to trip locations if missing
Existing dedupe behavior prevents duplicate trip entries

items.title: Make itinerary transit summaries clickable with popup details
acceptance: Optimized itinerary shows a single clickable transit summary per segment instead of inline leg detail text
Clicking the transit summary opens a popup window with notice, timing, transfer, wait, walk, and per-leg stop detail
Transit summary affordance has a visible hover state without affecting non-transit itinerary rendering

items.title: Fix OTP REST fallback local departure-time formatting
acceptance: Legacy OTP REST fallback sends local wall-clock date and time instead of UTC-sliced values
REST fallback departure queries stay aligned with the schedule time used by optimization
GraphQL-first probing behavior remains unchanged

items.title: Preflight managed OTP port conflicts and expose startup diagnostics
acceptance: Managed desktop OTP startup fails fast with an explicit port-conflict error before spawning OTP
Non-zero OTP exits include the latest process log detail in the surfaced error message
Settings UI still shows the latest OTP log line even when an error message is present

items.title: Preflight desktop OTP graph/runtime compatibility and allow artifact overrides
acceptance: Managed desktop OTP startup rejects incompatible graph serialization ids before spawning OTP
Desktop runtime supports overriding the managed OTP jar path or URL plus serialization id for custom compatible builds
Desktop OTP documentation explains the graph/runtime compatibility requirement and override path

items.title: Default OTP base URL to localhost and persist effective setting
acceptance: OTP base URL defaults to http://localhost:8080/ when no env or browser override is present
The effective OTP base URL is restored from browser storage on reload
Transit service and Settings UI use the same fallback behavior

items.title: Only auto-zoom map when generating a schedule
acceptance: Clicks after a schedule exists do not retrigger itinerary map fit/zoom
Map still recenters when a new optimized schedule is generated
Direct map focus actions continue to work independently of itinerary auto-zoom

items.title: Add cancellable optimization loading overlay
acceptance: Optimization overlay shows a cancel action while itinerary generation is running
Cancel stops the active optimization run from applying stale itinerary or route results
A new optimize action safely replaces any previous in-flight optimization

items.title: Add UI transparency control to Settings
acceptance: Settings includes a transparency control for floating planner UI surfaces
Chosen opacity persists across reloads via browser storage
Planner windows and dock respond to the configured opacity without affecting the map

items.title: Toggle itinerary transit segment visibility on map
acceptance: Each clickable transit segment summary includes a map-visibility toggle
Map respects per-segment visibility so overlapping transit segments can be isolated
Hiding all segment routes does not reintroduce the combined fallback route unexpectedly

items.title: Animate visible route path from schedule window
acceptance: Schedule window includes an animate-route action
Map animates along the currently visible route path when triggered
Animation respects per-segment visibility so only visible routes are animated

items.title: Make 100% UI transparency fully opaque
acceptance: UI transparency defaults to 100% on first load
At 100%, planner windows and dock fully obscure the map beneath them
Lower transparency settings still make the floating UI translucent

items.title: Make planner windows draggable, resizable, and better staggered
acceptance: Settings window is draggable like the other workspace windows
Workspace windows are resizable
The itinerary window opens offset from the Settings window when both are created together

items.title: Harden restored session locations before optimization
acceptance: Restored trip and custom locations are normalized and deduplicated before use
Legacy stored numeric fields such as duration and priority are coerced safely
Optimization behaves predictably after loading locations from a previous session

items.title: Use a direction arrow for route animation
acceptance: Animated route playback uses an arrow instead of a neutral circle
Arrow heading updates to match travel direction along the path
Animation still works for the currently visible route path only
